title: Context Engineering for Financial Services
desc: Context engineering is emerging as the foundational discipline for AI deployment in financial services — moving beyond prompt design to structured, auditable context management at scale.

title: Lessons for Enterprise AI Deployments
desc: Hard-won lessons from enterprise AI deployment projects across financial services: what fails in production, what scales, and how to structure the organizational journey.

title: MCP: The USB-C Standard for AI
desc: The Model Context Protocol positions itself as a universal connector for AI systems — a standardized integration layer that could reshape how financial institutions link AI models to their internal data and workflows.

title: FX Spillover: Digital Asset Market Impact
desc: Digital asset market volatility increasingly transmits to traditional FX markets. This brief quantifies the spillover dynamics and their implications for institutional risk frameworks.

title: Multi-Agent Systems for Financial Institutions
desc: Multi-agent AI architectures are moving from research labs to financial institution operations, with implications for compliance workflows, trade execution, and client service delivery.

title: SWIFT's Shift to Blockchain Infrastructure
desc: SWIFT's transition from blockchain experimentation to production-ready shared ledger infrastructure marks a structural turning point for global finance and the institutions that depend on it.

title: DTCC vs SWIFT: Solving Interoperability
desc: The world's most systemically important financial market intermediaries have identified network fragmentation as the single greatest barrier to digital asset adoption at scale — and are acting on it.

title: The Infrastructure Convergence Map
desc: Visual map of the convergence between AI, tokenization, and programmable settlement infrastructure — the three forces simultaneously reshaping financial market structure.

title: The Convergence Economy
desc: Two cost inputs — cognitive labor and transaction coordination — are being compressed by AI and programmable settlement infrastructure, generating a structural economic surplus.

title: Three Trends, One Infrastructure Stack
desc: The IMF's research confirms stablecoins and tokenization are a transmission channel with measurable effects on traditional finance — and that their infrastructure is converging into a single stack.

title: Private Credit Tokenization: The Next Frontier in RWA
desc: The private credit market exceeding $2.5 trillion is rapidly emerging as a credible infrastructure play for tokenization, with custody, compliance, and liquidity rails taking shape.

title: Leveraging AI as a Strategic Advantage
desc: A strategic framework for financial institutions building AI capability: where AI generates durable competitive advantage, where it commoditizes quickly, and how to allocate resources accordingly.

title: Strategic Imperative of Stablecoins for Cross-Border Payments
desc: JPMorgan, Visa, Mastercard, and regional banks are making stablecoin capabilities central to competitive positioning in cross-border payment corridors.

title: SEC Provides Clarity on Tokenized Securities
desc: On January 28, 2026, three SEC divisions issued a joint statement clarifying how federal securities laws apply to tokenized securities — a milestone removing a key barrier to institutional adoption.

title: Growth in Digital Asset Posts on LinkedIn
desc: Analysis of growth in digital asset-related LinkedIn posts, decomposed into tokenization/RWA, stablecoins, and FMI disintermediation — tracking institutional narrative momentum in real time.

title: The Hidden Plumbing of Stablecoins
desc: U.S. dollar stablecoins have crossed into mainstream financial infrastructure, but the GENIUS Act solves only part of the stability problem. The real constraints are systemic and operational.

title: x402: The Payment Infrastructure of Agentic AI
desc: As AI agents execute financial transactions autonomously, the x402 protocol represents a fundamental shift in how value moves across the internet — and who controls it.
